location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (marketplace/algorithmic pricing, people manager of PMs)

seniority.level: Director / Group PM (people manager of PMs)
level_signals: 8+ years of product management or relevant experience
2+ years of experience managing product managers
Manages a team of PMs across four sub-teams
Base range $208K-$260K in San Francisco, consistent with senior people-manager PM role
Interfaces with Lyft's executive team; owns P&L-relevant metrics (Gross Bookings, EBITDA)

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management or relevant experience, with significant time spent on algorithmic, data-driven, or marketplace products
must_haves.qualifier: significant time on algorithmic, data-driven, or marketplace products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2+ years of experience managing product managers
A deep technical understanding of microeconomics, and ideally a background in Operations Research, Machine Learning, or algorithmic systems
Demonstrated experience managing and growing a high performing team of product managers while providing development, mentorship, and excitement to exceed team and department goals
Experience working in deep product domains — using data and experimentation to guide a large cross-functional team towards a long-term vision
Strong communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills across a diverse group of stakeholders, from partner teams to executive levels
Ability to dive deep into complex technical concepts, but also abstract them to executives, stakeholders, operations, and legal
Maintain a strong POV on what to build for users, amidst many different stakeholders with different goals and world-class scientists + engineers on the team with their own opinions
Experience running A/B tests and using common statistical techniques to evaluate results, including developing post-launch testing strategies
Passion for Lyft and what we are trying to achieve in sustainable urban transportation

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: ideally a background in Operations Research, Machine Learning, or algorithmic systems

scope.team_size_estimate: 4 sub-teams, each with at least one PM; exact headcount unclear
scope.surface: End-to-end pricing lifecycle: Planned Pricing, Dynamic Pricing & Offer Selection, Rider Engagement, Price Orchestration
core_responsibilities: Manage and grow a team of product managers, providing development, mentorship, and coaching
Drive the strategic product and technology vision for Pricing, owning impact on Gross Bookings, Active Riders, and Adjusted EBITDA Margin
Set and align on business goals for pricing optimization with executives and business teams
Incorporate end-to-end impact of pricing decisions on riders, drivers, and network growth
Define short-term and long-term strategy, setting team success metrics and roadmap
Develop and execute a product roadmap via close collaboration with senior leadership and cross-functional partners
Review how PMs translate customer and user needs into functional and technical requirements
Guide data science, analysts, and engineers to review scope, prioritize projects, and define new opportunities
Serve as an inspiring communicator in team all-hands and executive reviews

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($208K-$260K base, SF)
Equity and bonus explicitly mentioned in addition to base
Clear ownership of a high-impact, strategic product domain (primary economic engine)
Detailed description of four sub-teams gives genuine scope clarity
18 weeks paid parental leave and strong benefits package
Hybrid schedule clearly specified (Mon/Wed/Thu in office)
red_flags: Hybrid role requires 3 days/week in office in San Francisco — eliminates remote candidates
Very high bar: 8+ years PM experience plus 2+ years managing PMs plus deep algorithmic/microeconomics knowledge
Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for international candidates

The 'ideally a background in Operations Research, Machine Learning, or algorithmic systems' is listed in the required section but framed as 'ideally', creating ambiguity about how hard this requirement actually is

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, team scope, and people-management scope; 'Group PM II' title suggests senior director-equivalent)
scope.team_size_estimate (exact headcount not stated; inferred from four sub-teams)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and JD context)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The Operations Research / Machine Learning / algorithmic systems background is listed within the main 'Experience' bullet that begins 'A deep technical understanding of microeconomics' with 'ideally' qualifying only the background portion — it is partially hard (microeconomics) and partially soft (OR/ML). Microeconomics understanding captured as must_have (hard); OR/ML background captured as nice_to_have (soft).
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned at all; marked as 'unclear' rather than 'open'.
